title: Rulership (horse)

([Japanese](/wiki/Japanese_language "Japanese language"): **ルーラーシップ**, foaled May 15, 2007) is a retired Japanese [Thoroughbred racehorse](/wiki/Thoroughbred_racehorse "Thoroughbred racehorse") and stallion best known for his victories in the 2012 [Queen Elizabeth II Cup](/wiki/Queen_Elizabeth_II_Cup "Queen Elizabeth II Cup"), [American Jockey Club Cup](/wiki/American_Jockey_Club_Cup "American Jockey Club Cup"), 2010 [Naruo Kinen](/wiki/Naruo_Kinen "Naruo Kinen"), 2011 [Nikkei Shinshun Hai](/wiki/Nikkei_Shinshun_Hai "Nikkei Shinshun Hai"), and [Kinko Sho](/wiki/Kinko_Sho "Kinko Sho").[[1]](#cite_note-jbis-1)[[2]](#cite_note-netkeiba-2)

**Rulership** ([Japanese](/wiki/Japanese_language "Japanese language"): **ルーラーシップ**, foaled May 15, 2007) is a retired Japanese [Thoroughbred racehorse](/wiki/Thoroughbred_racehorse "Thoroughbred racehorse") and stallion best known for his victories in the 2012 [Queen Elizabeth II Cup](/wiki/Queen_Elizabeth_II_Cup "Queen Elizabeth II Cup"), [American Jockey Club Cup](/wiki/American_Jockey_Club_Cup "American Jockey Club Cup"), 2010 [Naruo Kinen](/wiki/Naruo_Kinen "Naruo Kinen"), 2011 [Nikkei Shinshun Hai](/wiki/Nikkei_Shinshun_Hai "Nikkei Shinshun Hai"), and [Kinko Sho](/wiki/Kinko_Sho "Kinko Sho").[[1]](#cite_note-jbis-1)[[2]](#cite_note-netkeiba-2)

His name translates to "Position of Ruler, Authority of Governance."[[3]](#cite_note-jra-3)

## Racing career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rulership_(horse)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Racing career")]

### Before debut

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rulership_(horse)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Before debut")]

Rulership was born to the legendary mare [Air Groove](/wiki/Air_Groove "Air Groove"), by [Tony Bin](/wiki/Tony_Bin "Tony Bin"), who won the 1996 [Yushun Himba](/wiki/Yushun_Himba "Yushun Himba") (Japanese Oaks) and 1997 [Tenno Sho](/wiki/Tenno_Sho "Tenno Sho") (Autumn), earning her the 1997 [Japanese Horse of the Year](/wiki/Japanese_Horse_of_the_Year "Japanese Horse of the Year") title. As a [broodmare](/wiki/Mare "Mare"), her first [foal](/wiki/Foal "Foal") was [Admire Groove](/wiki/Admire_Groove "Admire Groove"), by [Sunday Silence](/wiki/Sunday_Silence "Sunday Silence"), winner of the [Queen Elizabeth II Cup](/wiki/Queen_Elizabeth_II_Cup_(Japan) "Queen Elizabeth II Cup (Japan)"). Rulership, her second foal, sired by [King Kamehameha](/wiki/King_Kamehameha_(horse) "King Kamehameha (horse)"), the 2004 [Tōkyō Yūshun](/wiki/T%C5%8Dky%C5%8D_Y%C5%ABshun "Tōkyō Yūshun") (Japanese Derby) winner, was highly anticipated due to his elite bloodline.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Owned by Sunday Racing Co., Ltd., he was syndicated for [¥](/wiki/%C2%A5 "¥")180 million (¥4.5 million × 40 shares).[[5]](#cite_note-5) At nine months old, he weighed 470 kg. He began training at [Northern Farm](/wiki/Northern_Farm_(stud) "Northern Farm (stud)") in August 2008 but developed swelling in his right hind hock in February 2009.

### 2009: two-year-old season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rulership_(horse)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: 2009: two-year-old season")]

Rulership debuted on December 27, 2009, in a 2-year-old [maiden race](/wiki/Maiden_race "Maiden race") at [Hanshin Racecourse](/wiki/Hanshin_Racecourse "Hanshin Racecourse") over 2000 meters of turf under jockey [Yasunari Iwata](/wiki/Yasunari_Iwata "Yasunari Iwata"). Starting as the favorite, he won by three and a half lengths over the runner-up.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

### 2010: three-year-old season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rulership_(horse)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 2010: three-year-old season")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a4/Rulership20101204.jpg/250px-Rulership20101204.jpg)](/wiki/File:Rulership20101204.jpg)

2010 Naruo Kinen

He opened 2010 as the favorite in the Wakagoma Stakes on January 23. Despite a troubled stretch run, he lost by one and a half lengths to Hiruno d'Amour, finishing second.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

Planned starts in the Saintpaulia Sho and Sumire Stakes were canceled due to fever and phlegmon, respectively. He instead contested the Armeria Sho on March 7 as the favorite. Despite interference from Tamuro Sky, who veered inward in the stretch, he rallied to win by one and a half lengths.[[8]](#cite_note-8) In the [Mainichi Hai](/wiki/Mainichi_Hai "Mainichi Hai"), a poor start and a neighboring horse’s breakdown led to a fifth place finish behind Danon Chantilly, dashing his [Satsuki Shō](/wiki/Satsuki_Sh%C5%8D "Satsuki Shō") (Japanese 2000 Guineas) aspirations.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

After a brief break, he returned in the [Principal Stakes](/wiki/Principal_Stakes "Principal Stakes") on May 8 with jockey [Norihiro Yokoyama](/wiki/Norihiro_Yokoyama "Norihiro Yokoyama"), substituting for Iwata. Closing with a field-fastest final 600m in 33.7 seconds, he won by four lengths, earning a Tokyo Yushun (Japanese Derby) entry.[[10]](#cite_note-10) In the Tokyo Yushun, ridden by Hirofumi Shii, he tracked mid-pack in a good position but lost momentum after being cut off in front by Gestalt, finishing fifth behind [Eishin Flash](/wiki/Eishin_Flash "Eishin Flash").[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12)

He returned to race in the [Naruo Kinen](/wiki/Naruo_Kinen "Naruo Kinen") on December 25 under Iwata, defeating Hiruno d'Amour by half a length for his first [group](/wiki/Graded_stakes_race "Graded stakes race") win.[[13]](#cite_note-13) He concluded the year with a 6th-place finish in the [Arima Kinen](/wiki/Arima_Kinen "Arima Kinen") under [Christophe Lemaire](/wiki/Christophe_Lemaire "Christophe Lemaire").[[14]](#cite_note-14)

### 2011: four-year-old season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rulership_(horse)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2011: four-year-old season")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/25/Rulership20110116.jpg/250px-Rulership20110116.jpg)](/wiki/File:Rulership20110116.jpg)

2011 Nikkei Shinshun Hai

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/55/Rulership20110528%282%29.jpg/250px-Rulership20110528%282%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Rulership20110528(2).jpg)

2011 Kinko Sho

Rulership’s 2011 campaign began with the [Nikkei Shinshun Hai](/wiki/Nikkei_Shinshun_Hai "Nikkei Shinshun Hai") on January 16, ridden by [Umberto Rispoli](/wiki/Umberto_Rispoli "Umberto Rispoli"). Despite initial plans to skip the race, he dominated as the second favorite, defeating [Rose Kingdom](/wiki/Rose_Kingdom "Rose Kingdom") and Hiruno d'Amour by two lengths for his second group title.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

In the [Dubai Sheema Classic](/wiki/Dubai_Sheema_Classic "Dubai Sheema Classic") with [Christophe Soumillon](/wiki/Christophe_Soumillon "Christophe Soumillon"), he broke off to a good start positioned up front early but faded to 6th in the final straight.[[16]](#cite_note-16) Returning to Japan, he won the [Kinko Sho](/wiki/Kinko_Sho "Kinko Sho") on May 28 under [Yuichi Fukunaga](/wiki/Yuichi_Fukunaga "Yuichi Fukunaga"), overcoming a slow start to catch Captain Thule in the stretch.[[17]](#cite_note-17)

In the [Takarazuka Kinen](/wiki/Takarazuka_Kinen "Takarazuka Kinen") on June 26, ridden by Norihiro Yokoyama, he started as the second favorite. In the race, he closed from the rear but lacked his usual acceleration, finishing 5th.[[18]](#cite_note-18) Skipping the Tenno Sho (Autumn) due to hoof issues,[[19]](#cite_note-19) he ended the year with a 4th-place finish in the [Arima Kinen](/wiki/Arima_Kinen "Arima Kinen") under [Ioritz Mendizabal](/wiki/Ioritz_Mendizabal "Ioritz Mendizabal"), clocking the fastest final 600m in 33.2 seconds.[[20]](#cite_note-20)

### 2012: five-year-old season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rulership_(horse)&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: 2012: five-year-old season")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/39/Rulership20120324.jpg/250px-Rulership20120324.jpg)](/wiki/File:Rulership20120324.jpg)

2012 Nikkei Sho

Rulership started his five-year-old season in the [American Jockey Club Cup](/wiki/American_Jockey_Club_Cup "American Jockey Club Cup") on January 22 under Yuichi Fukunaga. Rallying from the rear, he won by three lengths over Nakayama Knight for his fourth graded stakes title.[[21]](#cite_note-21) In the [Nikkei Sho](/wiki/Nikkei_Sho "Nikkei Sho") on March 24, he finished 3rd behind Neko Punch, who led wire-to-wire.[[22]](#cite_note-22)

On April 29, he triumphed in the [Queen Elizabeth II Cup](/wiki/Queen_Elizabeth_II_Cup "Queen Elizabeth II Cup") in [Sha Tin Racecourse](/wiki/Sha_Tin_Racecourse "Sha Tin Racecourse"), [Hong Kong](/wiki/Hong_Kong "Hong Kong"). Tracking third early, he drew clear in the stretch to win by 3.75 lengths over Thumbs Up, securing his first group one title.[[23]](#cite_note-23) The race featured elite rivals, including [Mehl-Mülhens-Rennen](/wiki/Mehl-M%C3%BClhens-Rennen "Mehl-Mülhens-Rennen") (German 2000 Guineas) winner [Irian](/wiki/Irian_(horse) "Irian (horse)") and [Irish Derby](/wiki/Irish_Derby "Irish Derby") winner [Treasure Beach](/wiki/Treasure_Beach_(horse) "Treasure Beach (horse)"), who was trained by [Aidan O'Brien](/wiki/Aidan_O%27Brien "Aidan O'Brien"). His strong performance was praised not only in Hong Kong but also in Europe.[[24]](#cite_note-24)

In the Takarazuka Kinen on June 24 under [Craig Williams](/wiki/Craig_Williams_(jockey) "Craig Williams (jockey)"), he closed from mid-pack but lost by two lengths to [Orfevre](/wiki/Orfevre "Orfevre").[[25]](#cite_note-25) In the Tenno Sho (Autumn) on October 28, he rallied with a field-fastest final 600m (33.1 seconds) but finished 3rd behind Eishin Flash.[[26]](#cite_note-26)

In the [Japan Cup](/wiki/Japan_Cup "Japan Cup") on November 25, despite a slow start and a blistering final 600m (32.7 seconds), he finished 3rd behind [Gentildonna](/wiki/Gentildonna "Gentildonna") and Orfevre.[[27]](#cite_note-27) His final start, the Arima Kinen on December 23, was marred by a 10-length delay at the gate after he stood up in the gate. Rallying from last, he managed to catch up with the pack, finishing 3rd behind [Gold Ship](/wiki/Gold_Ship "Gold Ship") and Ocean Blue. This was his third consecutive third-place finish.[[28]](#cite_note-28)[[29]](#cite_note-29)

Rulership retired on December 26, 2012, to stud at [Shadai Stallion Station](/wiki/Shadai_Stallion_Station "Shadai Stallion Station").[[30]](#cite_note-30)

## Stud record

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rulership_(horse)&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Stud record")]

His first crop in 2013 included 208 mares. Notable progeny include Ibuki who was his first winner in 2016 and Kiseki who won the 2017 [Kikuka-shō](/wiki/Kikuka-sh%C5%8D "Kikuka-shō"), his first group one winner.[[34]](#cite_note-34) He ranked 12th in 2017, 8th in 2018, and 5th in 2019 on the JRA Leading Sire list.

## In popular culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rulership_(horse)&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: In popular culture")]

An [anthropomorphized](/wiki/Anthropomorphism "Anthropomorphism") version of Rulership appears in *[Umamusume: Pretty Derby](/wiki/Umamusume:_Pretty_Derby "Umamusume: Pretty Derby")*, voiced by Hikari Senga.[[36]](#cite_note-36)

## Pedigree

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rulership_(horse)&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Pedigree")]

Pedigree of Rulership (JPN), bay colt, 2007[[37]](#cite_note-37)

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Sire [King Kamehameha](/wiki/King_Kamehameha_(horse) "King Kamehameha (horse)") b. 2001 | [Kingmambo](/wiki/Kingmambo "Kingmambo") (USA) b. 1990 | [Mr. Prospector](/wiki/Mr._Prospector "Mr. Prospector") | [Raise a Native](/wiki/Raise_a_Native "Raise a Native") |
| [Gold Digger](/wiki/Gold_Digger_(horse) "Gold Digger (horse)") |
| [Miesque](/wiki/Miesque "Miesque") | [Nureyev](/wiki/Nureyev_(horse) "Nureyev (horse)") |
| Pasadoble |
| Manfath (IRE) dk.b. 1991 | [Last Tycoon](/wiki/Last_Tycoon "Last Tycoon") | [Try My Best](/wiki/Try_My_Best "Try My Best") (USA) |
| Mill Princess |
| Pilot Bird (GB) | [Blakeney](/wiki/Blakeney_(horse) "Blakeney (horse)") |
| The Dancer (FR) |
| Dam [Air Groove](/wiki/Air_Groove "Air Groove") b. 1993 | [Tony Bin](/wiki/Tony_Bin "Tony Bin") (IRE) b. 1983 | Kampala (GB) | [Kalamoun](/wiki/Kalamoun "Kalamoun") |
| State Pension |
| Severn Bridge (GB) | Hornbeam |
| Priddy Fair |
| Dyna Carle | [Northern Taste](/wiki/Northern_Taste "Northern Taste") (CAN) | [Northern Dancer](/wiki/Northern_Dancer "Northern Dancer") |
| Lady Victoria |

| Peroxide (GB) (Family: 8-f)[[38]](#cite_note-38) |

* His dam Air Groove won the 1996 [Yushun Himba](/wiki/Yushun_Himba "Yushun Himba") and 1997 [Tenno Sho](/wiki/Tenno_Sho "Tenno Sho") (Autumn). Granddam Dyna Carle also won the Yushun Himba.
* His half-sister Admire Groove by Sunday Silence won the 2003 [Queen Elizabeth II Cup](/wiki/Queen_Elizabeth_II_Cup_(Japan) "Queen Elizabeth II Cup (Japan)").
* His half-brother Forgettable by Dance in the Dark won the 2009 [Stayers Stakes](/wiki/Stayers_Stakes "Stayers Stakes") and the 2010 [Diamond Stakes](/wiki/Diamond_Stakes_(Japan) "Diamond Stakes (Japan)").
* His half-sister Gullveig by Deep Impact won the 2012 [Mermaid Stakes](/wiki/Mermaid_Stakes_(Japan) "Mermaid Stakes (Japan)").
